page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and dedicated Intel Xeon 6455B processors. Operating at a high clock speed of 3.9 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor includes advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the r7iz.2xlarge demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, achieving a Geekbench single-core score of 2226.0 and a bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 14.85 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 34.55 GB/sec. Multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall within the average range, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8490.0 and uncached large-block memory read speeds of 70.75 GB/sec. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r7iz.2xlarge offers a high-performance memory profile suitable for workloads requiring rapid single-threaded execution alongside substantial memory capacity. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs makes it a specialized choice where external block storage is utilized. This hardware configuration is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, text processing, and workloads utilizing Intel AMX for CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.2xlarge is a memory-optimized, high-performance instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6455B processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB per core. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2226.0 and strong single-threaded compression performance. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that benefit from high CPU clock speeds, such as database operations, text processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier single-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a high-frequency 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized instance featuring DDR5 memory and advanced Intel matrix extensions for demanding single-threaded and database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6455B processor with 8 vCPUs and a 3.9 GHz clock speed
64.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and decompression tests
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix math
No local storage or GPU, utilizing a 3 Gbps baseline network
Optimized for database operations, text processing, and memory-heavy tasks
